event_blocks.summary: A woman with long dark hair, dressed in a white robe, begins her day in a softly lit bedroom, reflecting by a mirror on her commitment to end procrastination and recommit to her 2024 health goals, emphasizing consistency. Gentle, melancholic piano music accompanies her transition to the kitchen, where the whirring of a blender signals the preparation of a smoothie with oranges and other ingredients. She then moves to write in a white-covered notebook at a table before making her bed in a pink-lit room, an action noted by text overlays as an easy productive start. The sound of brushing teeth marks her morning hygiene routine in a dimly lit bathroom. Subsequently, the distinct sound of boiling water is heard as she operates a white SMEG kettle with gold lettering. While the water heats, she cleans the kitchen and prepares a large red mug adorned with white snowflake patterns, into which she pours 'Only Goodness Chicken Broth' from a white packet and a golden liquid from a small bottle. Finally, still in her white robe, she settles at a wooden table, journaling in her notebook next to the red mug, with a small vase of white flowers and a tablet positioned nearby, cultivating a calm, intentional atmosphere of self-care and productivity amidst warm lighting and minimalist decor.

Golden Liquid Bottle.description: A small bottle with a golden liquid, likely honey or oil, added to the mug.

Table Setting.description: A wooden table with a vase of white flowers, a tablet, and the red mug, creating a serene morning workspace.

Woman.description: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a white lace top and matching pants, later seen in a white robe; she performs morning routines including making her bed, brushing teeth, cleaning, and journaling. She speaks in a reflective and motivational tone about her year-end goals, consistency, and health routine.

event_blocks.summary: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a black puffer jacket, is seated in the driver's seat of a dimly lit car interior, describing the cold and foggy weather outside. She greets the viewers with a tired but friendly "Good morning, you guys," explaining she is in her garage and feels "so freezing." Holding a beige tube-like object with bright red nail polish on her hands, she speaks to the camera, gesturing with the object and sharing her exhaustion, feeling like she's "going in slow motion" despite it being Monday. She considers reaching the car a "win" and outlines her plan to drive to the gym to "work out," admitting they will be "just going through the motions today." The woman reveals her intention to "get my life back on track, back together for the new year," clarifying this is a prelude to a "2024 reset video" as she aims to "start now implementing the habits" for the new year and ensure she is "keeping up with everything" and "getting my life" in order.

raw_segments: The video opens with a woman, featuring long dark hair styled in two braids and wearing a black 'white fox' top, who states, "Let's do a little quick montage of me cleaning this." Upbeat, chill electronic music begins as she actively tidies a cluttered room, bending down to sort through a disorganized pile of black, white, red, and gray Scattered Clothes on the white floor near a White Wicker Chair and a white dresser, with audible rustling sounds as she places items into a Cardboard Box. On-screen text reads 'LET'S GET CLEANING BABY.' She then moves to a white Closet, opening its doors with an audible sound to reveal neatly folded clothes and hanging garments, indicating a transition from chaos to order. The scene shifts to a white Vanity area, where she clears away various beauty products, including a floral-patterned makeup bag, and wipes the surface clean with audible wiping sounds. On-screen text notes, 'this vanity needs a lot of work.' Finally, she holds up a pair of white New Balance Sneakers with gray accents, speaking about them. She identifies them as her "go-to runners" that she wears "all the time," noting they are "pretty dirty" and "not like really nice and white anymore." She explains her plan to clean them in the washing machine with towels on a normal cycle, followed by air drying, based on "Joel's" advice, as she's "been wanting to clean these runners for a while." The bright, minimalistic environment with white walls and natural light reinforces a clean, fresh atmosphere.

The video opens with a woman, her dark braided hair framing her face and wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text, holding up a pair of white New Balance sneakers. She speaks in a clear, engaging voice, explaining that the material of these 'runners' cleans up 'really, really well' in a washing machine, and she plans to put them in to see if it cleans them, even considering using 'Shout' to make them 'extra white.' She then moves to a bright, minimalist laundry area, placing the sneakers into a white Samsung washing machine, adding detergent from a large 'ECOS' bottle with a distinct pouring sound, and closing the door, all while an upbeat, light background music begins to play. The scene transitions to a clean kitchen with white cabinets and a gray backsplash, where the woman, her red-painted nails visible, holds a clear plastic container of fresh rosemary. She discusses her goal to take better care of her hair in 2024, stating that 'Rosemary' is 'the best natural hair growth and hair remedy ever,' and expresses her intention to make 'rosemary water' consistently, despite past inconsistencies.

The video opens with a top-down view of a black speckled saucepan and a clear plastic container of fresh rosemary labeled 'Greenway Farms Organic Rosemary' on a white countertop. A hand with red-painted nails opens the container, accompanied by a distinct rustling/crinkling sound, and places several sprigs of rosemary into the saucepan. A Woman's clear, conversational voice begins, introducing her 'little concoction' and explaining it's 'so easy to make,' noting she 'eyeballed a ratio' and doesn't follow a specific recipe. She describes the rosemary as smelling 'so good' and 'Christmassy.' The saucepan, which she refers to as 'a little pot,' is then moved to a gas stove and placed over a lit blue flame, beginning to heat the rosemary. The scene transitions to a low-angle shot of the Woman, wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text and her dark hair styled in two braids, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. She continues to narrate the process, instructing to fill the pot with water, transfer it to the stove, 'wait for it to boil,' let it boil for '5 minutes maybe or 10 minutes,' then 'turn the heat off' and let it soak in the water until it cools, before straining it into a spray bottle. She explains the ratio is to make it 'really potent' for spraying on 'your roots.' The Woman then shifts topics, mentioning she's about to go grocery shopping to make 'a couple of health things' to 'prep myself for going into this new year' and 'boost my immunity' because 'everybody has been getting sick' and 'there's something going around,' making it 'the season to get sick.'

The video opens with a woman, her long dark hair styled in two braids, wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' embroidered on the chest, and red-painted nails. She stands in a bright, minimalist white interior, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures and animated facial expressions. She explains in a clear, conversational voice that she plans to make ginger turmeric lemon orange shots from a TikTok recipe later today, for which she has ordered small shot glasses from Amazon. She also mentions needing to go grocery shopping and run errands, noting that it's a gloomy, rainy day, which makes it hard to get motivated, especially at the start of the new year. She adds that she's wearing a cozy sweatsuit for comfort, as she needs to persist despite the weather.

The scene unfolds inside a car, where a woman with long dark hair styled in two braids, wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text, speaks directly to the camera. She gestures expressively with her bright red-polished hands, noting the "pouring" rain outside and mentioning she's heading to the grocery store. She lists ingredients like ginger, turmeric, cayenne pepper, lemon, orange, avocados, and yams for "shots" she's making. At one point, she holds up a beige tube object, resembling a lip balm, demonstrating it by twisting the cap. She then picks up her smartphone, which has a black case with a dotted pattern, and shows the screen to the camera, explaining that her "notes app" contains an ongoing grocery list she checks weekly. The woman finds grocery shopping on a rainy day "cozy" and notes it's unusual as it hasn't rained much recently, contrasting it with a "dry November." Her demeanor is animated as she expresses a strong desire for a "white Christmas," recalling previous years with snow and hoping for it again, all while bright, overcast daylight illuminates the car's interior.

The segment opens with a woman, her long dark hair in two braids, speaking directly to the camera from inside a car, expressing, "let's go shopping." An upbeat, chill lo-fi track then begins, accompanying a montage of her grocery trip. The video opens with a woman, featuring long dark hair styled in two braids and wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text, speaking directly to the camera in a bright, minimalist indoor setting. event_blocks.summary: A woman with long dark hair in two braids, wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text, stands in a modern kitchen with white cabinetry and a stainless steel refrigerator, as upbeat, slightly jazzy music plays. She retrieves an 'Avalon Dairy Ltd. 117 Years 1906–2023' glass bottle from the refrigerator and places it on a white countertop next to a clear ribbed glass. With red-painted nails, she unscrews the silver cap and explains her intention for an eggnog taste test, pouring the creamy beige liquid into the glass. She comments on the reusability of the glass bottle for homemade milk, appreciating its size. After pouring, she sips the eggnog, describing it as "so thick" and "overbearing," and clarifies that while she enjoys eggnog lattes, she wouldn't drink it straight, noting that "Joel just eats this up." The scene shifts to her opening a white pantry cabinet, where the on-screen text 'pantry organization' appears as she begins to rearrange items on shelves containing baskets, tea boxes, jars, and bananas. She explains her goal to clean and reorganize the cabinet, which serves as their primary pantry, because she has groceries to put away and hasn't organized it in a while, aiming to make it look "nicer and more pleasant" as items are currently "thrown in here." The background music fades back in as the segment concludes.

raw_segments: The video opens with a woman, her long dark hair styled in two braids and wearing a black long-sleeve top with 'white fox' text, standing in a modern kitchen featuring white cabinetry and a stainless steel refrigerator. Upbeat, slightly jazzy background music plays softly. She approaches the refrigerator, opens it, and retrieves a large glass bottle labeled 'Avalon Dairy Ltd. 117 Years 1906–2023'. She places the bottle on a white countertop next to a clear ribbed glass and, with red-painted nails, unscrews the silver cap. As she prepares, her clear, conversational voice explains, "I thought we can do a little eggnog taste test together. I'm just gonna have a bit to try it." She pours the creamy beige liquid into the glass, noting, "I also really like how this one came in a glass jar 'cause I can reuse it when I make my homemade milk. It's always nice having extra glass jars and this one's like a really nice big one." After pouring, she sets the bottle down, then takes a sip, remarking, "Oh my god, it's so thick. That is so thick. So overbearing." She clarifies her preference, stating, "I'm not a huge fan of eggnog. I like a good eggnog latte, but I would never just drink it like this," adding that "Joel just eats this up." The scene transitions to her opening a white pantry cabinet, revealing neatly organized shelves with baskets, tea boxes, jars, and bananas. The on-screen text 'pantry organization' appears as she reaches in to adjust items. She explains her task, "Here in this cabinet, it's kinda got a little bit messy. So I want to clean it up, get it a little bit rearranged and clean it out 'cause I haven't done this in a while 'cause I still have some like pantry groceries to put away. This is like what we use as our pantry. So, I'm gonna organize it a bit and just make it look nicer and more pleasant 'cause things are just kind of thrown in here right now." The background music then fades back in as the segment concludes.

event_blocks.summary: In a warmly lit bedroom, a woman in a white lace-trimmed camisole and matching pants makes her bed amidst soft rustling sounds. She then moves to the kitchen, pouring a dark liquid from a TheraBreath bottle into a white SMEG kettle, accompanied by clinking sounds. As upbeat, slightly electronic music begins, she adds a Bone Broth packet to a brown ceramic mug with white heart patterns, followed by the kettle's boiling hum and a click as it turns off. She pours hot water into the mug, creating a final clink, and carries it to a wooden dining table. Sitting, she writes in a notebook with audible pen scratching, while on-screen text displays 'back to the grind today' and 'starting the day with gratitude = positive mindset'. The scene shifts to a car interior at night; now wearing a white puffer jacket, she zips it with a clear sound, applies red lipstick with a smacking sound, and holds a smartphone. Speaking directly to the camera, she explains her rush due to snoozing her alarm three times, mentioning her excitement for a power core class at Oxygen Yoga and Fitness, and her struggle with grogginess despite over eight hours of sleep. The narrative transitions to a dimly lit, warm orange-hued workout room where she, in a white sports bra and matching leggings, performs squats and lunges with a small black dumbbell, accompanied by upbeat R&B/hip-hop music with a female vocalist and on-screen text like 'LET'S DO THIS!'. Finally, in a bright kitchen, she prepares a smoothie by pouring Organic Coconut Water into a blender, adding Perfect Protein powder and Strawberry Yogurt, with audible pouring and blending sounds over the continuing music.

She identifies this as "Plant of Life 100% pure and natural essential rosemary oil," emphasizing its potent, good quality, which she can smell even through the packaging. She explains that this rosemary oil is too strong for direct scalp application and needs to be mixed with a carrier oil, choosing castor oil for its hair growth benefits. She then picks up a dark glass bottle with a white label and black cap, which she clarifies is an old, empty "The Ordinary" multi-peptide serum bottle. Demonstrating its dropper cap by unscrewing it, she explains her intention to use this bottle to store her hair concoction, gesturing to her head to suggest its use for hair care, and noting this will save her from remaking it every time. The background features a frosted glass shower door and a white wall-mounted shelf holding various colorful skincare products, maintaining a clean, organized aesthetic throughout her personal product demonstration.

event_blocks.summary: In a warmly lit bathroom, a woman with long dark hair, dressed in a white ribbed long-sleeve top, holds a black smartphone with a dotted case and a bottle of Sky Organics Organic Castor Oil. She explains her intention to apply the castor oil to her scalp overnight, followed by washing it out post-workout. Consulting her smartphone, she determines the correct ratio for rosemary oil to carrier oil, stating "five drops of rosemary oil with 10 milliliters of your carrier oil" and noting her 60-milliliter bottle. After a moment of humorous mathematical struggle, she calculates she needs "30 drops" and prepares to mix the concoction. Rustling sounds and a clink are heard as she unscrews the castor oil bottle, revealing a dropper, and begins pouring the oil, commenting on its ease of pouring and "chunky" consistency. She then states she will add "20 drops of the rosemary oil," counting aloud "one, two" before correcting to "26 drops." The sound of liquid mixing is audible as she combines the ingredients. The video concludes with a floral-patterned end screen displaying "subscribe" and "all socials @michaelabento," set to upbeat synth-pop music.
